content: Just as the double meaning of 'reflexion' as both optical reflection and human deliberate self-reflection frames how we think about metacognition, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can draw a new lesson from this duality: we can split LLM self-reflection into two sequential stages: first, an automatic 'optical reflection' stage that mirrors all inconsistent outputs from different solving perspectives back to the model, then a deliberate 'cognitive reflection' stage that evaluates these inconsistencies to refine responses. This split addresses current limitations of single-perspective self-reflection by systematically surfacing blind spots.
